MELItem.Title: Power Electronics Cooling System (PECS) Aft Section

ListItem.Para: Leak is verified to be in aft section of the loop.
Associated isolation valve is deactivated closed.
Associated fluid level is verified to be adequate.
Associated ram air fan operates normally.

Note.Para: The aft section of the PECS loop is that part of the loop downstream of the isolation valve and associated with the SCUs and the lav/galley ventilation heat exchanger.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify that leak is in aft section of the loop and confirm isolation valve is deactivated closed (AMM DDG 21-81-01).

ListItem.Para: Verify leak is in aft section of PECS loop.
If the caution message LIQUID COOLING L/R does not appear, the leak has been isolated and is in the aft section of the PECS loop.
Clean any fluid and fluid residue on any airplane structure, components or fluid lines.
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For leak in left loop, LIQUID CLG SOV VLV-L.
For leak in right loop, LIQUID CLG SOV VLV-R.

Note.Para: Opening the circuit breaker will cause the associated LIQUID CLG PRESS SNSR L/R to appear. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 21-81-04.
The LIQUID COOLING ISLN VLV L or R status message will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm associated isolation valve state is ISOLATED.
If valve state is not ISOLATED, gain access to supply shutoff valve.
Turn the open/close knob until the valve is in the close position.
Verify associated PECS loop fluid level is adequate, or service as required.

OperationNote.Para: Closing the isolation valve will deactivate the SCUs associated with the affected PECS loop and may degrade GCM and recirculation cooling air performance.
